title: Attention-Based Graph Neural Networks for Molecular Property Prediction
abstract: We introduce a graph attention architecture that predicts quantum-chemical properties of small molecules directly from their atomic graphs. By weighting neighbor contributions with learned attention coefficients, the model captures long-range electronic effects that fixed message-passing schemes miss. On the QM9 benchmark we reduce mean absolute error on the HOMO-LUMO gap by 14 percent relative to prior graph convolutional baselines, and we show the attention maps recover chemically meaningful bonding patterns.
authors: A. Petrov, L. Schmidt, R. Nakamura

title: Self-Supervised Pretraining of Vision Transformers on Astronomical Survey Images
abstract: Large sky surveys produce millions of unlabeled galaxy images. We pretrain a vision transformer with a masked-patch reconstruction objective on 4 million cutouts from the Dark Energy Survey, then fine-tune for morphological classification with only a few hundred labels. The pretrained features transfer across instruments and reduce the labeling budget needed to reach expert-level accuracy by an order of magnitude.

title: A Renormalization-Group Analysis of Holographic Entanglement Entropy
abstract: We study the flow of entanglement entropy under renormalization in strongly coupled conformal field theories using the holographic Ryu-Takayanagi prescription. We derive a monotonicity theorem for the entanglement c-function in three bulk dimensions and connect it to the null energy condition. Our results clarify how information is reorganized across scales in gauge-gravity duality.

title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion Improves Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain QA
abstract: Dense retrievers and sparse lexical retrievers make complementary errors: dense models capture semantics but miss rare terms, while BM25 anchors on exact token matches. We combine their ranked lists with reciprocal rank fusion and observe consistent gains on Natural Questions and TriviaQA without any additional training. We analyze when fusion helps and provide a simple rule of thumb for the fusion constant.
authors: D. Ivanova, P. Müller, J. Okonkwo

title: Sentence-Level Chunking Strategies for Long-Document Retrieval
abstract: Splitting long documents into chunks is a hidden hyperparameter of retrieval-augmented systems. We compare fixed-token windows, sentence-boundary chunks, and overlapping sliding windows across three scientific corpora. Overlapping windows recover the most relevant passages at the cost of redundancy, while sentence-based chunks give the best precision for short factual queries. We provide guidance for choosing a strategy under a fixed embedding budget.

title: Superconducting Qubit Coherence Limits from Two-Level System Defects
abstract: We characterize the dominant decoherence channel in transmon qubits fabricated on sapphire substrates. Spectroscopy of avoided level crossings reveals a bath of two-level systems in the amorphous oxide at the metal interface. We model the resulting frequency noise and propose a surface-treatment recipe that extends T1 coherence times by a factor of three.

title: Contrastive Document Embeddings for Citation-Aware Scientific Search
abstract: We learn document-level embeddings of scientific papers using a contrastive objective built from the citation graph: papers that cite each other are pulled together in embedding space. The resulting representation supports nearest-neighbor search that respects topical relatedness even when abstracts share few words. We evaluate on a held-out set of citation-prediction tasks and recommendation rankings.

title: Turbulent Energy Cascades in Two-Dimensional Quantum Fluids
abstract: Using a Gross-Pitaevskii simulation of a driven Bose-Einstein condensate, we observe an inverse energy cascade reminiscent of classical two-dimensional turbulence. We quantify the spectral exponents and show that quantized vortices cluster into large-scale coherent structures. The findings bridge classical fluid dynamics and superfluid hydrodynamics.

title: Efficient Fine-Tuning of Large Language Models with Low-Rank Adapters
abstract: Full fine-tuning of billion-parameter language models is prohibitively expensive. We study low-rank adaptation, which freezes the base weights and learns small rank-decomposition matrices injected into attention layers. Across instruction-following and summarization benchmarks we match full fine-tuning quality while training under one percent of the parameters and cutting GPU memory in half.

title: Constraints on Dark Matter Self-Interaction from Galaxy Cluster Collisions
abstract: Merging galaxy clusters offer a natural laboratory for probing the particle nature of dark matter. We analyze gravitational-lensing maps of eight colliding systems and bound the dark-matter self-interaction cross-section per unit mass. Our limits disfavor a subset of hidden-sector models proposed to resolve small-scale structure anomalies.

title: Neural Operators for Solving Parametric Partial Differential Equations
abstract: We train a neural operator that maps boundary conditions and coefficient fields directly to PDE solutions, generalizing across a family of elliptic and parabolic problems. Once trained, inference is three orders of magnitude faster than a classical finite-element solver at comparable accuracy. We demonstrate the method on heat conduction and Darcy-flow benchmarks.

title: Hybrid Lexical-Semantic Retrieval for Patent Prior-Art Search
abstract: Patent search demands both exact terminology matching and conceptual coverage. We build a hybrid system that runs BM25 over patent claims and a dense bi-encoder over abstracts, then fuses the two ranked lists. On an examiner-annotated benchmark the hybrid recovers prior art that each component alone misses, improving recall at rank 20 by nine points.

title: Topological Edge Modes in Photonic Crystal Waveguides
abstract: We design a photonic crystal that hosts topologically protected edge states at telecom wavelengths. The bulk band structure carries a nontrivial Chern number, and we observe robust unidirectional propagation around sharp corners with negligible backscattering. The platform enables compact integrated photonic routing immune to fabrication disorder.

title: Measuring Semantic Drift in Word Embeddings Across Scientific Decades
abstract: Scientific vocabulary shifts as fields mature. We align word embeddings trained on physics abstracts from successive decades and quantify how the meaning of terms like entanglement and field migrate over time. The method surfaces emergent subfields and offers a diagnostic for tracking conceptual change in the literature.

title: A Variational Quantum Eigensolver for Lattice Gauge Theories
abstract: We present a variational quantum algorithm that prepares ground states of a Z2 lattice gauge theory on near-term hardware. By encoding gauge constraints directly into the ansatz we avoid costly penalty terms. Numerical experiments on a small lattice match exact diagonalization and suggest a path toward simulating confinement on quantum processors.

title: Calibrated Uncertainty Estimates for Medical Image Segmentation
abstract: Deployment of segmentation networks in clinical settings requires trustworthy uncertainty. We compare deep ensembles, Monte Carlo dropout, and evidential deep learning on MRI tumor delineation. Evidential models give the best-calibrated pixelwise confidence while remaining a single forward pass, which matters for real-time intraoperative use.

title: The Cosmic Microwave Background Bispectrum and Primordial Non-Gaussianity
abstract: We forecast constraints on local-type primordial non-Gaussianity from upcoming CMB experiments using an optimized bispectrum estimator. Accounting for lensing-induced contamination, we show that the parameter f_NL can be measured to better than unity, sharpening tests of single-field inflation.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Scientific Question Answering
abstract: We build a question-answering system over a corpus of physics papers that retrieves supporting passages and conditions a language model on them. We study how chunk size, retriever choice, and reranking affect factual accuracy. Hybrid retrieval combined with a cross-encoder reranker reduces hallucinated citations substantially.

title: Anomalous Transport in Strongly Correlated Electron Systems
abstract: We measure thermal and electrical conductivity in a family of layered correlated metals and find a violation of the Wiedemann-Franz law over a wide temperature range. A holographic strange-metal model reproduces the observed scaling, suggesting that quantum criticality governs transport in these materials.

title: Benchmarking Vector Databases for Billion-Scale Nearest Neighbor Search
abstract: We evaluate several approximate nearest-neighbor indexes including HNSW, IVF-PQ, and disk-based ANN on billion-vector workloads. We report the recall-latency-memory trade-offs and provide practical guidance on choosing an index given a similarity metric and a memory budget. Cosine and inner-product metrics behave differently under quantization, which we analyze in detail.

title: Diffusion Models for Inverse Problems in Computational Imaging
abstract: We use a pretrained diffusion model as a learned prior to solve ill-posed imaging inverse problems such as deblurring and compressed sensing. By guiding the reverse diffusion with the measurement likelihood we reconstruct high-fidelity images without task-specific training. The approach outperforms classical regularizers on several modalities.

title: Gravitational Wave Memory from Binary Black Hole Mergers
abstract: The nonlinear gravitational-wave memory effect produces a permanent displacement of test masses after a merger. We compute the memory waveform for precessing binaries and assess its detectability with next-generation interferometers. A confident detection would test the nonlinear structure of general relativity.

title: Few-Shot Named Entity Recognition for Materials Science Literature
abstract: Extracting material names, properties, and synthesis conditions from papers enables automated knowledge bases. We adapt a prompt-based language model to recognize domain entities from only a handful of annotated examples per type. The system reaches competitive F1 with two orders of magnitude less labeled data than supervised baselines.

title: Scaling Laws for Contrastive Vision-Language Pretraining
abstract: We empirically characterize how downstream zero-shot accuracy of contrastive image-text models scales with dataset size, model size, and compute. We find smooth power-law trends and identify a compute-optimal allocation between encoder capacity and data. The fitted laws predict performance of models an order of magnitude larger.

title: Phase Transitions in Random Constraint Satisfaction Problems
abstract: We study the satisfiability threshold of random k-SAT using the cavity method from statistical physics. We locate the clustering and condensation transitions that precede the satisfiability threshold and connect them to the failure of local search algorithms. The analysis sharpens the picture of algorithmic hardness near the phase boundary.

title: An Empirical Study of Tokenization Effects on Multilingual Retrieval
abstract: Subword tokenization choices interact with retrieval quality, especially for morphologically rich and low-resource languages. We compare byte-level, unigram, and BPE tokenizers across a multilingual passage-retrieval benchmark and quantify how vocabulary fragmentation degrades dense retrieval. We recommend tokenizer settings that balance shared cross-lingual subwords against fragmentation.

title: Magnetic Reconnection and Particle Acceleration in Solar Flares
abstract: We simulate magnetic reconnection in the solar corona with kinetic particle-in-cell methods and track nonthermal electron acceleration. The simulations reproduce the hard X-ray spectra observed during flares and constrain the fraction of magnetic energy converted to energetic particles.

title: Graph Transformers with Structural Positional Encodings
abstract: Plain transformers ignore graph structure when positional information is absent. We inject Laplacian eigenvector and random-walk positional encodings into a transformer and achieve strong results on molecular and citation-graph benchmarks. We analyze which encodings help on which graph families.

title: Measuring the Hubble Constant with Strongly Lensed Quasar Time Delays
abstract: Time delays between multiple images of a strongly lensed quasar provide an independent route to the Hubble constant. We model six lens systems with hierarchical Bayesian inference and report a value in mild tension with early-universe estimates. We discuss systematic uncertainties from the lens mass profile.

title: Sparse Autoencoders Reveal Interpretable Features in Language Models
abstract: We train sparse autoencoders on the activations of a transformer language model and recover monosemantic features corresponding to human-interpretable concepts. We show that steering these features causally changes model behavior, offering a handle on mechanistic interpretability and model editing.

title: Critical Casimir Forces Between Colloidal Particles in a Binary Solvent
abstract: Near the critical point of a binary liquid mixture, solvent fluctuations induce effective forces between immersed colloids. We measure these critical Casimir forces with optical tweezers and compare their range and sign to field-theoretic predictions, confirming universality of the scaling functions.

title: Cross-Encoder Reranking Versus Bi-Encoder Retrieval: A Cost-Quality Analysis
abstract: Bi-encoders index documents independently and scale to large corpora, while cross-encoders jointly attend over query and document for higher accuracy at higher cost. We quantify the quality gap and the latency cost across several retrieval benchmarks and propose a two-stage pipeline that retrieves with a bi-encoder and reranks the top candidates with a cross-encoder.

title: Floquet Engineering of Topological Bands in Driven Optical Lattices
abstract: Periodic driving can imprint nontrivial topology onto otherwise trivial band structures. We propose a driving protocol for ultracold atoms in an optical lattice that realizes an anomalous Floquet topological insulator, and we describe a measurement of the associated edge transport.

title: Long-Context Language Models Struggle with Information Buried in the Middle
abstract: We probe how language models use information placed at different positions within long input contexts. Across several models, retrieval accuracy is highest when relevant facts appear at the beginning or end and degrades sharply in the middle. We connect this to attention dilution and discuss chunking and reordering mitigations for retrieval-augmented pipelines.

title: Neutrino Mass Ordering from Atmospheric Oscillation Data
abstract: We combine atmospheric neutrino oscillation measurements with reactor constraints to determine the neutrino mass ordering. A joint fit mildly favors the normal ordering and tightens the allowed range of the mixing angle theta_23. We outline how next-generation detectors will resolve the ordering decisively.

title: Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search Under Distribution Shift
abstract: ANN indexes are typically built once and queried for a long time, but data distributions drift. We study how recall degrades as the query distribution diverges from the indexed corpus and propose lightweight reindexing triggers. Our analysis covers cosine and Euclidean metrics and quantifies when normalization protects against drift.

title: Equivariant Neural Networks for Molecular Dynamics Simulation
abstract: We build an SE(3)-equivariant network that predicts interatomic forces and accelerates molecular dynamics while preserving physical symmetries. The learned potential conserves energy over long trajectories and generalizes across molecule sizes, enabling simulations at near ab-initio accuracy with classical-MD speed.

title: Bootstrap Bounds on Conformal Field Theory Operator Dimensions
abstract: We apply the numerical conformal bootstrap to constrain the spectrum of operator dimensions in three-dimensional conformal field theories. By imposing crossing symmetry and unitarity we carve out the allowed region and isolate the critical Ising model as a kink on the boundary.

title: Domain-Adaptive Pretraining Improves Biomedical Passage Retrieval
abstract: General-purpose dense retrievers underperform on specialized biomedical queries. We continue pretraining a bi-encoder on PubMed abstracts with a contrastive objective and observe large gains on biomedical retrieval benchmarks. We analyze how much of the improvement comes from vocabulary coverage versus task-specific signal.

title: Quantum Error Correction with the Surface Code Below Threshold
abstract: We report a demonstration of the surface code in which increasing the code distance reduces the logical error rate, crossing the fault-tolerance threshold. We characterize the dominant error sources and extrapolate the physical-qubit overhead required for a practical fault-tolerant quantum computer.
